decisions.reasoning: Post-WWDC 2026 selloff (~8% since June 8) is classic sell-the-news: Siri AI launched in beta only (US-only, Europe/China excluded), disappointing elevated expectations. However Q2 revenue was +17% YoY to $111.2B and iPhone +22% show strong underlying fundamentals. Morgan Stanley raised PT to $360 from $330 on June 9 maintaining Buy. SMA signal shows long (fast > slow) though price $295.81 is below both SMAs indicating near-term weakness. Holding on intact thesis.

sources.headline: Apple stock price slides 8% after WWDC 2026 despite AI progress and raised price target
AAPL Stock Slides Following WWDC, But Analysts Broadly Raise Targets

decisions.invalidation: Siri AI fails to drive upgrade cycle; China revenue falls another 10%+ YoY; EU/US regulatory action on Gemini-Siri deal

decisions.reasoning: Stock down 17% YTD vs S&P +7%, notable underperformance. SMA signal flat — fast SMA 410.54 crossed below slow SMA 416.74, negative momentum. Insider sale today: Takeshi Numoto sold 4,500 shares (~$1.8M, Form 4 filed June 15) is mild negative signal. Long thesis intact: Azure cloud growth, Copilot enterprise adoption, defense contracts, upcoming new coding model. Analyst 12-month consensus PT $518 implies ~30% upside. Position small ($1,196 MV). Holding but on watch.

sources.headline: Microsoft Stock Is Trailing the Market in 2026 — Here s Why It s a Screaming Buy Right Now
Takeshi Numoto Sells 4,500 Shares of Microsoft

decisions.invalidation: Azure growth decelerates sharply; Copilot ROI questioned by enterprises; SMA gap widens with no fundamental catalyst

decisions.reasoning: SMA flat signal reflects sector-wide rout after Broadcom Q3 AI revenue guide missed by $1.2B on June 5 (reported $16B vs ~$17.2B expected), wiping $1.3T from chip sector. Selloff is sector-driven not NVDA-specific: NVDA Q1 FY27 was exceptional (revenue $81.6B +85% YoY, EPS $1.87 vs $1.77 est). 62 analysts Strong Buy with average PT $298.93 (+43% upside from $209). Bear risks: CUDA moat erosion from custom silicon, AMD share gains, Rubin architecture delay. Holding on fundamental strength.

sources.headline: 3 Semiconductor Stocks to Buy While They Are Down in June
PHLX chip index drops 10% on Broadcom AI revenue guide miss

decisions.invalidation: Custom silicon captures >15% GPU workloads from hyperscalers; Rubin delay extends past Q4 2026; AI capex cycle decelerates

decisions.reasoning: Only profitable position (+2.81% unrealized). SMA long signal with fast SMA 744.28 above slow SMA 742.22. Broad market supported by AI tailwinds and resilient earnings. Mixed macro (some recession risk, unemployment rising, only 17% of S&P stocks outperforming index) but near-term momentum positive. No reason to exit.

sources.headline: 2026 Mid-Year Outlook: U.S. Stocks and Economy

decisions.invalidation: Macro deteriorates sharply — unemployment spike, negative GDP; Fed pivots hawkish unexpectedly

summary: First session: all holds, no trades — near 95% gross long exposure cap ($8,964/$9,666), AAPL/MSFT/NVDA theses intact despite SMA headwinds; day P/L +$159.79 (+1.68%)
open_theses: AAPL: post-WWDC dip is sell-the-news; AI upgrade cycle plays out over 2-4 quarters; watch Siri rollout updates and China revenue trend
MSFT: underperforming market significantly but Azure/Copilot fundamentals intact; flat SMA signal warrants close monitoring next session — may trim if momentum does not recover
NVDA: Broadcom-driven sector selloff is temporary; 85% revenue growth and Strong Buy consensus support holding through volatility; watch June 24 annual meeting
SPY: broad market anchor, long SMA signal, positive unrealized — hold as portfolio hedge
